title: huchimingia podocarpa
intro: Huchimingia podocarpa, also known as "Queñua", is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae, native to the Andean regions of South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Huchimingia podocarpa include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support digestive health. Huchimingia podocarpa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Andean medicine to make poultices and salves.
